etymology.description: 声(聲) (shēng, 'stone chime') + 殳(殳) (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') = a stone chime being struck
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸(qīng) visually represents a stone chime being struck with a mallet, capturing the essence of sound or resonance associated with metal or stone. It combines the concept of sound with the action of hitting, effectively portraying the intended meaning.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
